name: Grammarly
description: AI writing assistant for drafting customer support emails and internal documentation
justification: Grammarly processes text for grammar and style suggestions. Does not store proprietary data or require access to sensitive systems.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for email drafting and documentation. Block clipboard access for sensitive fields.

name: GitHub Copilot
description: AI pair programmer integrated into VS Code for code completion
justification: Copilot sends code snippets to GitHub servers. Exposes internal code patterns to a third party.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for open-source repos. Block for proprietary repositories containing trade secrets.

name: ChatGPT (GPT-4)
description: General-purpose LLM used by engineering for code review, brainstorming, and debugging
justification: General-purpose LLM with broad data handling. Can process PII, source code, and confidential prompts. Data retention policies vary.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for non-sensitive tasks. Block file uploads. Require quarterly compliance review.

name: Notion AI
description: Requested by Alice Chen from Engineering
justification: Notion AI is integrated directly into the organization's collaborative workspace, which often contains sensitive company documents, product roadmaps, and meeting notes. Since the tool is requested by an Engineering team member, there is a high likelihood of the AI interacting with proprietary source code and internal PII. Because Notion AI processes user inputs via third-party LLM providers, there is a risk of intellectual property exposure and data leakage if proper enterprise data privacy controls and model-training opt-outs are not active.
recommendedPolicy: Restrict access to users on an Enterprise plan with an active Data Processing Addendum (DPA) that guarantees organizational data is not used for model training. Implement automated data loss prevention (DLP) controls to prevent the input of secrets, API keys, or raw customer PII into Notion AI prompts.

retrievedNistContext.definition: Govern cultivates and implements a culture of risk management within organizations designing, developing, deploying, evaluating, or acquiring AI systems. It establishes processes to identify, understand, and communicate risk tolerance, and ensures risk management is integrated into organizational policies and procedures.

Map establishes the context to frame AI risks. It identifies intended use, deployment setting, stakeholders, and the potential positive and negative impacts of the AI system on people, groups, communities, organizations, and society.

Measure employs tools, techniques, and methodologies to analyze, assess, benchmark, and monitor AI risk and related impacts. It uses knowledge gathered from Map and prior Measure activities to evaluate risks for trustworthy characteristics.

Manage allocates risk resources to mapped and measured risks on an ongoing basis. It includes responding to, recovering from, and communicating about incidents or events, and plans for regular monitoring and improvement.

name: Perplexity Pro
description: AI research assistant with real-time web search for technical research
justification: Search queries are sent to external servers. May expose internal project names and research direction.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for general research. Block queries containing project codenames or internal URLs.

name: Midjourney
description: AI image generation for marketing materials and social media content
justification: Image prompts sent to external servers. Potential for brand/IP exposure through prompt leakage.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for marketing use. Require prompt review for client-facing content.

name: Claude Code
description: Anthropic's CLI coding assistant for development workflows
justification: Full codebase access with file system operations. Can read source code, configs, and environment variables containing secrets.
recommendedPolicy: Evaluate for engineering use only. Require IP audit before approval.

name: Jasper AI
justification: Handles marketing copy with potential customer PII and campaign financial data. Third-party data processing.
recommendedPolicy: Blocked pending legal review of data processing agreement.

name: Cursor
description: AI-first code editor with multi-model support
justification: Code editor that sends snippets to multiple LLM providers. Supports local models for sensitive repos.
recommendedPolicy: Allow with local model for proprietary code. Cloud model only for open-source.

name: DeepL
description: AI translation service for international communications
justification: Translation service with GDPR compliance. Text sent to servers but processed ephemerally.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for general translation. Block for legal/contract documents.

name: v0 by Vercel
description: Generative UI system to build frontends from natural language prompts
justification: Generative UI tool that processes layouts and output code. Exposes front-end styling and structure to Vercel/OpenAI systems.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for standard UI mocking. Restrict from uploading sensitive branding or trade secrets.

name: DeepSeek Coder
description: Open-source code completion model used in local IDEs for software development
justification: Open-source model processing code logic. Self-hosted options exist, but cloud version transfers IP to external servers.
recommendedPolicy: Self-host where possible. Restrict third-party API keys from proprietary workspaces.

name: OpenAI o1
description: Advanced reasoning model optimized for complex STEM problems and coding tasks
justification: High-capability reasoning model with broad scope. Can store and review advanced engineering, math, and internal corporate data.
recommendedPolicy: Allow only for authorized STEM/development teams. Keep files with proprietary algorithms blocked.

name: Phind
description: Developer search engine optimized for quick coding answers and troubleshooting
justification: General developer search. Queries are public/search queries but don't typically expose proprietary data structure.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for technical references. Block pasting large chunks of proprietary code in queries.

name: Sora by OpenAI
description: Text-to-video AI model for generating highly realistic video scenes
justification: Generates videos from text. Risk is primarily related to copyright leakage, brand misrepresentation, or data egress of scripts.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for marketing brainstorms. Restrict from uploading pre-release scripts or product designs.

name: Synthesia
description: AI video generation platform that creates videos with AI avatars from text
justification: Generates custom video assets with digital presenters. Potential risk is associated with synthetic content verification and data handling.
recommendedPolicy: Allow for training and onboarding videos. Require approval for client-facing content.

name: Harvey AI
description: Generative AI tool built specifically for legal research, document analysis, and contract drafting
justification: Processes highly confidential NDAs, client records, and legal briefs. Heavy exposure of sensitive customer agreements.
recommendedPolicy: Blocked pending final risk team legal validation and dedicated tenant contract approval.

name: Fireflies.ai
description: AI meeting assistant that transcribes, summarizes, and analyzes voice conversations
justification: Attends video conferences and records voices, transcriptions, and meeting notes. Broad exposure of internal board decisions.
recommendedPolicy: Blocked from client-facing meetings and confidential internal corporate reviews.

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
description: Advanced conversational LLM used for brainstorming and complex analysis
justification: Claude 3.5 Sonnet is a highly advanced general-purpose LLM. When utilized for complex analysis, users are highly likely to input proprietary source code, sensitive financial data, or PII to get high-quality outputs. Because data is transmitted to third-party servers, this creates significant risk of data leakage, intellectual property exposure, and compliance violations (such as GDPR or CCPA). Under the EU AI Act, general-purpose AI models require governance to manage systemic risks, and the NIST AI RMF highlights the need to continuously measure and manage third-party data processing risks and potential model hallucinations.
recommendedPolicy: Restrict access to enterprise-tier accounts with guaranteed data-privacy opt-outs (where prompts are not used for model training). Deploy browser-based Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tools to block the pasting of unmasked PII, financial ledgers, or raw cryptographic keys, and require mandatory training on safe LLM prompting.
